Title: Successful user login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuthentication service is active.
- User account exists with valid email/password.
Test Steps:
1. User enters email and password in the UI.
2. UI sends `signIn(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication validates credentials.
4. FirebaseAuthentication returns `Credenziali valide` and a token.
5. UI retrieves user data using the token.
6. UI displays confirmation of successful login.
Expected Result:
- User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard.
- Token and user data are stored locally.

Title: Failed login with invalid credentials
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuthentication service is active.
- User account does not exist or password is incorrect.
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid email/password in the UI.
2. UI sends `signIn(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication returns `Credenziali errate`.
4. UI displays error message: *"Errore login (es. Password errata)"*.
Expected Result:
- User remains on login screen.
- No token or user data is stored.

Title: Successful user registration with valid data
Preconditions:
- FirebaseAuthentication and FirebaseFirestore are active.
- No existing account with the provided email.
Test Data:
- Name: `John Doe`
- Email: `john.doe@example.com`
- Password: `NewPass789!`
- Nationality: `Italian`
- Role: `Citizen`
Test Steps:
1. User enters registration data in the UI.
2. UI sends `creaAccount(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication validates data and returns `Validazione dati`.
4. UI receives token and sends `salvaUtente(nome, email, token)` to FirebaseFirestore.
5. FirebaseFirestore confirms user save.
6. UI displays: *"Conferma registrazione avvenuta"*.
Expected Result:
- New user record is created in FirebaseFirestore with all attributes.
- User is automatically logged in.

Title: Failed registration with invalid email format
Test Data:
- Name: `Jane Doe`
- Email: `invalid-email` (missing @ and domain)
- Password: `ValidPass123!`
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid email in the UI.
2. UI sends `creaAccount(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication returns `[Dati non validi]`.
4. UI displays: *"Errore registrazione: Email non valida"*.
Expected Result:
- No user record is created.
- User remains on registration screen.

Title: Registration with weak password (edge case)
Test Steps:
1. User enters weak password in the UI.
2. UI sends `creaAccount(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication returns `[Dati non validi]`.
4. UI displays: *"Errore registrazione: Password troppo debole"*.
Expected Result:
- No user record is created.
- UI prompts to enter a stronger password.

Title: Successful report creation with image and entity assignment
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- FirebaseFirestore and FirebaseStorage are active.
- At least one area and category exist in the database.
Test Data:
- Description: `Broken streetlight on Maple Ave`
- Image: `streetlight.jpg` (valid file)
- Coordinates: `40.7128, -74.0060` (New York)
- Urgency: `High`
- Assigned Category: `Infrastructure`
- Assigned Area: `Manhattan`
Test Steps:
1. User enters report details (description, image, coordinates, urgency) in the UI.
2. UI uploads `immagine` to FirebaseStorage.
3. FirebaseStorage returns `URL immagine salvata`.
4. UI sends `Cerca ente competente più vicino(coordinate, urgenza)` to FirebaseFirestore.
5. FirebaseFirestore returns `Ente più vicino trovato (dati ente)`.
6. UI sends `salvaSegnalazione(datiSegnalazione + URL immagine + dati ente)` to FirebaseFirestore.
7. FirebaseFirestore confirms `Conferma salvataggio segnalazione`.
8. UI displays: *"Conferma invio segnalazione con ente assegnato"*.
Expected Result:
- Report record is created in FirebaseFirestore with:
  - `idUser`, `idCategorie`, `idArea`, `urgency`, `description`, and `imageURL`.
  - Assigned entity data is linked.
- Image is stored in FirebaseStorage.

Title: Report creation with missing image (edge case)
Test Data:
- Description: `Pothole on Oak St`
- Image: *(none selected)*
- Coordinates: `34.0522, -118.2437` (Los Angeles)
- Urgency: `Medium`
Test Steps:
1. User enters report details but skips image upload.
2. UI attempts to send `Carica immagine(immagine)` to FirebaseStorage.
3. UI detects no image and skips `URL immagine salvata`.
4. UI sends `salvaSegnalazione(datiSegnalazione)` **without** image URL.
5. FirebaseFirestore rejects save due to missing image.
6. UI displays: *"Errore: Carica un'immagine per completare la segnalazione"*.
Expected Result:
- No report is saved.
- User is prompted to add an image.

Title: Report creation with invalid coordinates (edge case)
Test Data:
- Description: `Graffiti on wall`
- Image: `graffiti.jpg`
- Coordinates: `999.9999, 999.9999` (invalid)
- Urgency: `Low`
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid coordinates in the UI.
2. UI sends `Cerca ente competente più vicino(coordinate, urgenza)` to FirebaseFirestore.
3. FirebaseFirestore returns error: *"Coordinate non valide"*.
4. UI displays: *"Errore: Inserisci coordinate geografiche valide"*.
Expected Result:
- No report is saved.
- User is prompted to correct coordinates.

Title: Report creation with non-existent category/area
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Database contains categories/areas, but selected `idCategorie`/`idArea` do not exist.
Test Steps:
1. User selects invalid category/area IDs via UI (e.g., hardcoded or API call).
2. UI sends `salvaSegnalazione(datiSegnalazione + URL immagine)` to FirebaseFirestore.
3. FirebaseFirestore validates foreign keys and rejects save.
4. UI displays: *"Errore: Categoria/Area non trovata"*.
Expected Result:
- No report is saved.
- User is prompted to select valid category/area.

Title: Verify report associations (Users → Categories → Areas → Reports)
Preconditions:
- Database contains:
  - 1 User (`id=1`, role=`Citizen`).
  - 1 Category (`id=1`, description=`Environment`).
  - 1 Area (`id=1`, location=`Downtown`, description=`Central`).
  - 1 Report (`id=1`, `idUser=1`, `idCategorie=1`, `idArea=1`).
Test Steps:
1. Query FirebaseFirestore to fetch:
   - User with `id=1` and their associated reports.
   - Category with `id=1` and its associated reports.
   - Area with `id=1` and its associated reports.
2. Verify:
   - Report `id=1` appears in User `id=1`'s reports.
   - Report `id=1` appears in Category `id=1`'s reports.
   - Report `id=1` appears in Area `id=1`'s reports.
Expected Result:
- All associations are correctly linked in the database.
- No orphaned records exist.

Title: Delete user and verify cascading effects on reports/notifications
Test Steps:
1. Delete User `id=1` via UI/admin interface.
2. Query FirebaseFirestore to verify:
   - Reports `id=1` and `id=2` are **soft-deleted** (or hard-deleted with `idUser` set to `null`).
   - Notification `id=1` is **soft-deleted** (or hard-deleted with `idUser` set to `null`).
3. Attempt to fetch User `id=1`'s reports/notifications.
Expected Result:
- User `id=1` is deleted.
- Associated reports/notifications are either deleted or marked as orphaned.
- Queries for User `id=1`'s data return empty results.

Title: User receives notification for their report
Preconditions:
- Database contains:
  - 1 User (`id=1`).
  - 1 Report (`id=1`, `idUser=1`, `urgency=High`).
  - Notification system is configured to send alerts for `High` urgency.
Test Steps:
1. System detects `urgency=High` for Report `id=1`.
2. System creates Notification:
   - `idUser=1`
   - `idReport=1`
   - `text="Your report has been flagged as high urgency. Action required."`
   - `date=current_timestamp`.
3. User logs in and checks their notifications.
Expected Result:
- Notification appears in the user's list.
- Notification links to the associated report (`idReport=1`).

Title: High-urgency report triggers immediate notification
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Notification system is active.
Test Data:
- Report details:
  - Urgency: `High`
  - Description: `Fire hazard detected`
  - Coordinates: `37.7749, -122.4194` (San Francisco)
Test Steps:
1. User submits report with `urgency=High`.
2. UI sends `salvaSegnalazione` to FirebaseFirestore.
3. System detects `urgency=High` and immediately:
   - Creates a notification for the user.
   - Sends an alert to the assigned entity (if applicable).
4. User receives notification within 5 seconds.
Expected Result: - Notification is created and delivered instantly
